weather.summary: Saturday: 11°C, overcast clouds. Sunday: 14°C, light rain

metadata.greeting: This weekend in London offers a variety of activities suitable for a 3-year-old, from exploring dinosaurs at Crystal Palace Park to enjoying hands-on experiences at the Science Museum. With a mix of indoor and outdoor options, you can plan for any weather. Don't miss the free events at museums and parks!

activities.title: Library Club at the Hackney Showroom
activities.description: Reading corner and diverse book-pairing service.
activities.whyItWorks: A relaxed environment for toddlers to explore books and stories. Drop-in so no need to book.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hackney Downs
transport.tip: Check term times before visiting.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Immersive play spaces known as Story Worlds and Story Garden. Baby Storytelling, crafts and singalong.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and engaging environment for young children. Plenty of space to move and explore.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Victoria Park
activities.description: East London's most famous park with playgrounds and green spaces.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of space to run around, playgrounds, and a relaxed atmosphere. Great for a picnic.

transport.fromHackney: Bus or Overground to Hackney Wick
transport.tip: Check for specific events on their Facebook page.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Interactive galleries designed for children, including Design, Imagine and Play. Mini Museum for under 2s.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits and dedicated spaces for younger children. Caters specifically to kids' interests.

transport.tip: Check for special events and workshops.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small, beautifully designed wooden playground with a pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for toddlers, offering a safe and engaging play space.

transport.tip: Located near the Olympic Park.

activities.title: The Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore the history of home and domestic life.
activities.whyItWorks: While not entirely geared towards toddlers, the museum offers engaging exhibits and gardens to explore.

transport.tip: Check out the gardens for a relaxing break.

activities.title: Discover Greenwich Peninsula
activities.description: Stroll along the colorful art trail, immerse yourself in nature at the Ecology Park.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides outdoor space to explore with interesting sights, perfect for toddlers.

transport.tip: Enjoy tasty food in the Canteen Food Hall and Bar.

activities.title: Half Moon Young People's Theatre
activities.description: Dedicated young people's theatre with shows and workshops.
activities.whyItWorks: Performances specifically designed for children, providing an engaging introduction to theatre.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance as shows are popular.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, mammals, and other fascinating exhibits. Easter Family Festival with drop-in talks and workshops.
activities.whyItWorks: Large, open spaces for toddlers to roam. Dinosaurs are always a hit! Free entry makes it accessible.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Liverpool Street, then Circle Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Arrive early to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Interactive exhibits for kids to discover how the world works. Wonderlab offers hands-on experiences.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden in the basement is perfect for toddlers, with water, light and sound exhibits. Free entry.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Liverpool Street, then Circle Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Book Wonderlab tickets in advance.

activities.title: V&A Museum
activities.description: Explore art and design from around the world. Daily free activities and events for children.
activities.whyItWorks: Backpacks of activities for kids to explore the museum. Large collections to spark curiosity.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Liverpool Street, then Circle Line to South Kensington
transport.tip: Check for family trails and hands-on discovery areas.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: Small zoo with rare species of birds and animals, including wallabies and lemurs.
activities.whyItWorks: Small and manageable for toddlers. Free entry and family-friendly things to do in the area.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Northern Line to Golders Green, then a short bus ride
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Crystal Palace Park Dinosaurs
activities.description: Explore the dinosaur sculptures and enjoy the playground and maze.
activities.whyItWorks: Dinosaurs! Plus a playground and open space for running around. Great for a picnic.

transport.tip: Visit the maze and lakes.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Explore sea travel, exploration, trade and naval history. All Hands Children's Gallery for under 8s.
activities.whyItWorks: Child-friendly activities and a dedicated play area for younger children. Free entry.

transport.tip: Check out the character encounters on Saturdays.

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: London's highest public garden with 360° views of the city.
activities.whyItWorks: Amazing views and greenery to explore. A unique experience for toddlers.

transport.tip: Book in advance as spaces are limited.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Explore London's history as a great port. Mudlarks soft play area for children under 8.
activities.whyItWorks: Dedicated soft play area for toddlers. Engaging exhibits for older children and adults.

transport.tip: Check for family trails around the museum.

activities.title: Voyage to the Deep
activities.description: An immersive exhibition exploring the wonders of the ocean depths.
activities.whyItWorks: Sensory experience for toddlers. The Horniman also has gardens and an aquarium.

transport.tip: Check for family events and workshops.

activities.title: I Want My Hat Back Trilogy
activities.description: Jon Klassen's beloved books come alive on stage.
activities.whyItWorks: Puppet show specifically designed for young children. Short and engaging.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Kew Gardens – Sounds of Blossom
activities.description: Explore the blossoming royal parks.
activities.whyItWorks: Outdoor walking, fresh air, space to run.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and Overground to Kew Gardens
transport.tip: Head for Cherry Walk and Magnolia Walk, then finish near The Orangery café.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers at Hobbledown Heath
activities.description: Easter Egg trail, indoor and outdoor play, and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Combination of animals, play, and special events.

transport.tip: Most families spend 4–5 hours.

activities.title: The Lion King
activities.description: Musical.
activities.whyItWorks: Visually stunning, but maybe too long for a 3 year old.

transport.tip: Book tickets in advance.

activities.title: Paddington The Musical
activities.description: Musical.
activities.whyItWorks: Popular children's character.

transport.tip: Pack marmalade sandwiches.

activities.title: Alice in Wonderland
activities.description: Theatre show.
activities.whyItWorks: Colourful and imaginative.

transport.tip: Check for family discounts.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun
activities.price: Some paid activities and some free
activities.description: Festival of fun and creativity for families including shows, musical adventures, amazing puppetry and lots of free fun.
activities.whyItWorks: Has a variety of activities from shows to creative spaces.

transport.tip: Visit the REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground (paid activity).
